title: Reports
lede: Every dollar and every dial, side by side. Campaign Reports shows what each campaign did and what it cost (or earned), and Campaign Stats is your live mission-control while campaigns run — plus a map of every other report in iMash.

steps.title: Open Campaign Reports
steps.content:
Campaign Reports is the billing-grade summary of your calling campaigns: one row per campaign, with calls, minutes, and money — perfect for month-end reviews and client invoicing.

  1. In the top navigation bar, click Campaigns.
  2. In the sub-tabs that appear underneath, click Campaign Reports.

Each row in the table is one campaign, so you can compare all your campaigns against each other in a single view.

💡 If you don't see the Campaigns tab, your account may not have campaigns enabled, or your role hides this page — ask your administrator.

images.alt: Campaign Reports page with the filter bar and per-campaign report table
images.caption: Campaign Reports: one row per campaign with calls, minutes, and billing

images.alt: Campaign Reports page with the filter bar and per-campaign report table
images.caption: Campaign Reports: one row per campaign with calls, minutes, and billing

steps.title: Set the date range and filters
steps.content:
The filter bar at the top controls exactly what the table (and any export) includes:

  • Filter by Account — main accounts with team members see this dropdown; pick one account to see only its campaigns. Clear it to see everyone.
  • Filter by Campaign — narrow the report to a single campaign. The list updates to match the selected account.
  • Start Date → End Date — the reporting window. Only calls inside this window are counted.
⚠️ The date range defaults to the last 30 days so the page loads fast. If a campaign shows zeros, its calls probably happened earlier — clear the date range (click the × that appears when you hover over it) to report on all time.

images.alt: Filter bar with Filter by Account, Filter by Campaign, and a date range picker
images.caption: Account, campaign, and date filters — the window defaults to the last 30 days

images.alt: Filter bar with Filter by Account, Filter by Campaign, and a date range picker
images.caption: Account, campaign, and date filters — the window defaults to the last 30 days

steps.title: Read the report columns
steps.content:
For every campaign the table shows:

  • Account Name / Account Email — who owns the campaign (useful when you manage team-member accounts).
  • Campaign ID — the campaign's unique ID, with a copy button for support tickets or API work.
  • Campaign — the campaign name.
  • Status — draft, active, inactive, or completed.
  • Calls/Events — how many calls the campaign placed in the selected window.
  • Minutes/Units — total billable talk time consumed.
  • Sell Rate — the per-minute price billed for this campaign.
  • Bill Amount — the total amount billed (minutes × sell rate).

Main accounts also see three profitability columns:

  • Cost Rate — what the calls actually cost per minute.
  • Total Cost — the total underlying cost.
  • Profit — Bill Amount minus Total Cost, so you instantly see which campaigns make you money.

Click any column header to sort by it (click again to flip the direction), and use the pagination controls under the table to page through large lists.

images.alt: Campaign Reports table showing account, campaign, status, calls, minutes, sell rate, bill amount, cost rate, total cost, and profit columns
images.caption: The full column set — Cost Rate, Total Cost, and Profit appear for main accounts

images.alt: Campaign Reports table showing account, campaign, status, calls, minutes, sell rate, bill amount, cost rate, total cost, and profit columns
images.caption: The full column set — Cost Rate, Total Cost, and Profit appear for main accounts

steps.title: Export the report to CSV
steps.content:
Take the numbers with you — into spreadsheets, BI tools, or client reports:

  1. Set the filters and date range first — the export respects exactly what you've filtered.
  2. Click Export to CSV in the top-right corner of the page.
  3. Your browser downloads a file named campaign-reports-<date>.csv (with a -filtered suffix when filters are active).
💡 The export isn't limited to the page you're looking at — it pulls up to 50,000 rows matching your filters, so you get the whole report in one file.

steps.title: Open Campaign Stats for live monitoring
steps.content:
Campaign Reports tells you what happened; Campaign Stats shows what's happening right now. It's the screen to keep open while your dialer is running.

  1. In the top navigation bar, click Campaigns, then the Campaign Stats sub-tab.
  2. Set Auto-refresh (top-right) to how often the screen should update: Off, 5 seconds, 15 seconds (default), 30 seconds, or 1 minute.
  3. Click Refresh Now any time you want an instant update.

images.alt: Campaign Stats page with live KPI tiles, spend and earnings cards, and the campaigns table
images.caption: Campaign Stats: a live dashboard of every campaign, refreshed automatically

images.alt: Campaign Stats page with live KPI tiles, spend and earnings cards, and the campaigns table
images.caption: Campaign Stats: a live dashboard of every campaign, refreshed automatically

steps.title: Read the live activity tiles
steps.content:
The tiles at the top summarize your whole dialing operation at a glance:

  • Active Campaigns — how many campaigns are currently running.
  • Calls In Progress — live calls connected right now.
  • Calls Today — total calls placed since midnight.
  • Calls/Min — the current dialing pace.
  • Queue Depth — contacts waiting to be dialed.
  • Pending in Queue — calls queued and about to go out.
  • Retry Eligible — contacts ready for another attempt.
  • Errors (Last Hour) — recent call errors; if this climbs, something needs attention.
  • Active Users — accounts with campaign activity right now.

images.alt: Live tiles showing Active Campaigns, Calls In Progress, Calls Today, Calls/Min, Queue Depth, Pending in Queue, Retry Eligible, Errors, and Active Users
images.caption: Live activity tiles — your dialing operation at a glance

images.alt: Live tiles showing Active Campaigns, Calls In Progress, Calls Today, Calls/Min, Queue Depth, Pending in Queue, Retry Eligible, Errors, and Active Users
images.caption: Live activity tiles — your dialing operation at a glance

steps.title: Track today's spend and earnings
steps.content:
Two money cards update live as calls complete:

  • Spend (Today) — what you actually spent today across your campaigns (only calls you paid for).
  • Earnings (Today) — what you earned today from markup on team-member calls plus any affiliate commissions.
💡 Team-member (sub) accounts see only Spend (Today) — the Earnings card and profit figures are reserved for main accounts.

images.alt: Spend (Today) and Earnings (Today) cards with live dollar amounts
images.caption: Spend and Earnings update live while campaigns run

images.alt: Spend (Today) and Earnings (Today) cards with live dollar amounts
images.caption: Spend and Earnings update live while campaigns run

steps.title: Drill into (and control) each campaign
steps.content:
The table at the bottom lists every campaign you can see, with live columns:

  • Campaign — the campaign name and owner.
  • Status — Running, Cooling Down, After Hours, Paused, Stopped, Completed, Draft, or Inactive.
  • Active Calls — live calls versus the campaign's concurrency limit (e.g. 0 / 3).
  • Progress — how far through the dial list the campaign is.
  • Calls — total placed, how many connected, and connected minutes.
  • Cost / Earnings — money spent and earned on this campaign today.
  • Working Hours — the daily window the campaign is allowed to dial in.
  • Actions — pause or resume the campaign right from the report.

To work the list:

  1. Type in Search campaigns... to find a campaign by name.
  2. Use Filter by status to see only Running, Paused, Completed, etc.
  3. Click the + at the start of a row to expand its dial-list breakdown: Contacts, Attempts Made, Attempts Left, Finished, and a Progress bar.
  4. Click Refresh above the table to reload the list instantly.

images.alt: Campaigns table with an expanded row showing Contacts, Attempts Made, Attempts Left, Finished, and Progress
images.caption: Expand any row for its dial-list breakdown; pause or resume from the Actions column

images.alt: Campaigns table with an expanded row showing Contacts, Attempts Made, Attempts Left, Finished, and Progress
images.caption: Expand any row for its dial-list breakdown; pause or resume from the Actions column

steps.title: Know where every other report lives
steps.content:
iMash reports on every part of the platform — each area has its own dedicated reporting screen:

  • Analytics Dashboard — account-wide call KPIs, charts, and call history filters. See Analytics Dashboard.
  • Contact Center Reporting — human-agent performance: answered calls, talk time, queue wait, per-agent stats. In the app: Contact Center → Reporting tab. See Contact Center Reporting.
  • Dispatch Reports — field-service finance and team reports: job revenue, technician payouts, statistics. In the app: Dispatch → Finance Reports / Team Reports / Statistics tabs. See Dispatch Reports.
  • Cost Management — your balance, pricing, and platform spend. See Cost Management.
  • Call Logs — the per-call record behind every report: recordings, transcripts, outcomes. See Call Logs.

Rule of thumb: campaign questions → this page; people questions → Contact Center Reporting; jobs and money in the field → Dispatch Reports; platform-wide numbers → Analytics Dashboard.

steps.title: Act on what you see
steps.content:
Reports are only as good as the decisions they trigger:

  • Low connect rate or slow Progress? Adjust the campaign's Working Hours and time zone so you reach people when they answer.
  • Spend climbing faster than results? Hit Pause in the Actions column, then refine the dial list or the AI agent's script.
  • Errors (Last Hour) rising? Check your phone numbers and provider health before burning through the list.
  • One campaign clearly out-earning the rest? Clone its recipe — same agent, same hours, new list — and scale what works.

That loop — measure, adjust, re-run — is how good campaigns become great ones.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
